title: Tapping for: Is It Selfish to Enjoy This?
subtitle: A tapping practice for the guilt that arrives when pleasure does
excerpt: A tapping practice for the guilt that arrives alongside enjoyment. The feeling that pleasure is unearned, or that enjoying your life is somehow wrong when others are struggling. The script gives back the permission to feel good.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when pleasure arrives with guilt attached, or when you find yourself pulling back from enjoyment before it fully lands.

content.text: A tapping practice for the guilt that arrives alongside enjoyment. The feeling that pleasure is unearned, or that feeling good is somehow wrong when others are not. The script names what is happening and gives back the permission to enjoy your own life.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I feel guilty for enjoying this when other people are struggling, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I keep pulling back from enjoyment before it fully arrives, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I do not know if I have earned the right to enjoy this, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** Is it selfish to enjoy this?
**Side of eye:** Other people are struggling. Should I be feeling good?
**Under eye:** I started to feel something good and then pulled back from it.
**Under nose:** Is it okay to feel joy when so much is hard?
**Chin:** I have been carrying a lot. The enjoyment felt wrong. Like I had not earned it.
**Collarbone:** Or like feeling good now means something bad later.
**Under arm:** The enjoyment keeps getting pulled back before it fully arrives.
**Top of head:** I want to feel good but there is this voice that says I should not.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** My joy does not take anything from anyone else.
**Side of eye:** Enjoying my life does not mean I am unaware of the world.
**Under eye:** I am allowed to feel good.
**Under nose:** Pleasure is not a betrayal.
**Chin:** I do not have to earn the right to enjoy my own life.
**Collarbone:** My enjoyment is evidence that I am here and present.
**Under arm:** I can feel this fully. That is allowed.
**Top of head:** I am allowed to enjoy my own life.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
